   151→/**
   152→ * Create page-specific metadata that merges with base metadata
   153→ *
   154→ * @example

   161→ */
   162→export function createMetadata(options: {
   163→  title: string;
   164→  description?: string;
   165→  path?: string;
   166→  image?: string;
   167→  noIndex?: boolean;
   168→}): Metadata {
   169→  const { title, description, path, image, noIndex } = options;
   170→  const url = path ? `${siteConfig.url}${path}` : siteConfig.url;
   171→  const ogImage = image || siteConfig.ogImage;
   172→
   173→  return {
   174→    title,
   175→    description: description || siteConfig.shortDescription,
   176→    ...(noIndex && {
   177→      robots: {
   178→        index: false,
   179→        follow: false,
   180→      },
   181→    }),
   182→    openGraph: {
   183→      title: `${title} | ${siteConfig.name}`,
   184→      description: description || siteConfig.shortDescription,
   185→      url,
   186→      images: [
   187→        {
   188→          url: ogImage,
   189→          width: siteConfig.ogImageWidth,
   190→          height: siteConfig.ogImageHeight,
   191→          alt: `${title} - ${siteConfig.name}`,
   192→        },
   193→      ],
   194→    },
   195→    twitter: {
   196→      title: `${title} | ${siteConfig.name}`,
   197→      description: description || siteConfig.shortDescription,
   198→      images: [ogImage],
   199→    },
   200→    alternates: {
   201→      canonical: url,
   202→    },
   203→  };
   204→}
   205→
   206→/**
   207→ * Create metadata for dynamic pages (e.g., course details, player profiles)
   208→ *
   209→ * @example
   210→ * // In a dynamic page [id]/page.tsx:
   211→ * export async function generateMetadata({ params }) {
   212→ *   const course = await getCourse(params.id);
   213→ *   return createDynamicMetadata({
   214→ *     title: course.name,
   215→ *     description: `Play ${course.name} - Par ${course.par}`,
   216→ *     path: `/courses/${params.id}`,
   217→ *   });
   218→ * }
   219→ */
   220→export function createDynamicMetadata(options: {
   221→  title: string;
   222→  description: string;
   223→  path: string;
   224→  image?: string;
   225→  type?: 'article' | 'profile' | 'website';
   226→  publishedTime?: string;
   227→  modifiedTime?: string;
   228→}): Metadata {
   229→  const {
   230→    title,
   231→    description,
   232→    path,
   233→    image,
   234→    type = 'website',
   235→    publishedTime,
   236→    modifiedTime,
   237→  } = options;
   238→
   239→  const url = `${siteConfig.url}${path}`;
   240→  const ogImage = image || siteConfig.ogImage;
   241→
   242→  return {
   243→    title,
   244→    description,
   245→    openGraph: {
   246→      title: `${title} | ${siteConfig.name}`,
   247→      description,
   248→      url,
   249→      type,
   250→      ...(publishedTime && { publishedTime }),
   251→      ...(modifiedTime && { modifiedTime }),
   252→      images: [
   253→        {
   254→          url: ogImage,
   255→          width: siteConfig.ogImageWidth,
   256→          height: siteConfig.ogImageHeight,
   257→          alt: title,
   258→        },
   259→      ],
   260→    },
   261→    twitter: {
   262→      title: `${title} | ${siteConfig.name}`,
   263→      description,
   264→      images: [ogImage],
   265→    },
   266→    alternates: {
   267→      canonical: url,
   268→    },
   269→  };
   270→}

# COMPREHENSIVE AUDIT OF LZG.HOME (FRONTEND)

## Executive Summary

The lzg.home directory is a modern Next.js 15 + React 19 frontend application for the Lazy Golfing golf tracking platform. It uses TypeScript (strict mode), Tailwind CSS 3.4, shadcn/ui components, and implements the Miami Vice dark/light theme. The application is built as a static export for Cloudflare Pages deployment.

## KEY ARCHITECTURAL PATTERNS

### 1. Single Source of Truth Pattern
- **Header/Footer:** Only in `src/app/layout.tsx`, never duplicated
- **Metadata:** Centralized in `src/lib/metadata.ts`
- **Colors:** Centralized in `src/lib/theme.ts`
- **SEO Schema:** Centralized in `src/components/seo/JsonLd.tsx`

### 2. Route Groups for Different Layouts
- `(auth)` group for login pages with minimal layout
- Main group for pages with full header/footer
- URL doesn't include group name: `/login` not `/(auth)/login`

### 3. Client vs Server Components
- Most components are server components (default)
- Client components marked with `'use client'`:
  - Header (uses usePathname)
  - ThemeToggle (uses useTheme)
  - ThemeProvider
  - LoginPage (uses useState, useEffect)

### 4. Metadata Factory Pattern
- `createMetadata()` for static pages
- `createDynamicMetadata()` for dynamic pages
- Automatic OpenGraph, Twitter Cards, canonical URLs

### 5. Component Composition
- Radix UI primitives for headless components
- shadcn/ui for pre-styled variants
- CVA (class-variance-authority) for variant management
